rawLeads.company_description:
Alderway is bridging the Equity Gap for brilliant businesses in often overlooked and underappreciated Backbone Sectors by providing the investment and support needed to unlock remarkable returns. 
 
We're a team of experienced investors, entrepreneurs and operators who have been involved in growing many successful businesses, including the groundbreaking success of Vanarama - the only vehicle leasing company to achieve an exit to a FTSE 100 giant. This is how we know building a successful business can be challenging but also very rewarding.
 
Our unique investing approach, Partnership Capital, ensures alignment of interests between us and the people we back. We not only provide financial investment but also offer support through our Value Creation Programme, empowering businesses to achieve their growth ambitions.
 
We Empower not Impose. We take a significant shareholding in the business, ensuring we have 'skin in the game,' while working alongside entrepreneurs. 
 
Our investments range from £3m to £25m, providing enough capital to fuel growth plans. 
 
If appropriate, we can facilitate early liquidity for entrepreneurs, allowing them to de-risk, and  focus on the mission rather than the mortgage while retaining a substantial stake for potential exit events. 

We invest only in businesses where our experience and guidance can accelerate growth. 
 
Our investment approach is Fundamentals Focused, emphasising profitability, cash generation, and a clean balance sheet.
 
Our ideal partners are innovative, scalable, lower mid-market (£10m - £50m revenue) and businesses not limited to the SE & London. 

By focusing on these fundamentals and partnering with businesses in Backbone Sectors, we aim to drive sustainable growth and deliver remarkable returns for all.

rawLeads.company_description:
"Investing with economic impact" is more than a no-brainer nowadays. In a time where "sustainable finance" and "impact startups" are on the rise, we as libertarians value facts, evidence and data over aspirations, projections and castles in the air. Social or environmental impact are not immaterial. But we believe that market economics should take center stage in solving some of the world's most pressing problems, for the betterment of both our planet (or of what we deem important about it) and the society. 

Our holding is currently invested in path-breaking startups in Switzerland, Panama, Malawi, and Canada. We're always on the lookout for 1) innovative, 2) scalable, and 3) economically compelling cases. If this holds for the venture you're building, please be in touch and send your pitch deck to: sebastian@hoffmann-economics.com

For investors, especially business angels, family offices and foundations: If you seek to realize a higher ROI and to support startups with a real-world impact in lieu of startups that produce mere hot air about how they will eventually (!) save the planet and/or society, then recall that "in the long run we're all dead" (John Maynard Keynes) and, particularly, that most startups vanish before they have a true impact. As entrepreneurs and company builders ourselves, we have seen startup teams from the inside which are black sheeps (often with the best intentions) and would have been rightly in the focus of shortsellers if they had been publicly listed already. We help you avoid such traps which would burn your capital without any positive long-term impact. Please be in touch as well (christian@hoffmann-economics.com) and learn how to apply principles of Austrian Economics to your investments.

rawLeads.company_description:
Almaz Capital was founded in 2008 to invest in early stage startups in the CIS (commonwealth of independent states) and CEE (central and eastern Europe) region with the backing of Cisco Systems and the EBRD (European Bank for Reconstruction and Development). Almaz Capital set out to invest and build a bridge between investee companies from the newly independent countries of the former Soviet Union to resources and connections in Silicon Valley. With that intention the firm opened its first offices in Silicon Valley and Moscow. During that period Almaz supported many startups with origins in Russia, such as QIK (acquired by Skype), Yandex (NASDAQ:YNDX) or Parallels (acquired by Corel).

In March 2014, after Crimea was annexed by Russia, Almaz Capital stopped investing in companies with any ties to Russia. In 2017 Almaz moved its European operations to Berlin and focus shifted from the countries of the CIS to the CEE region (central and eastern Europe). Following the crackdowns in Minsk in 2020 Almaz stopped investing in Belarus, another former Soviet state.

We are helping our Ukraine-based startups survive the war ravaging their country. Not only are we one of the largest investors in Ukraine over the last 5 years, but we are also a long-time supporter actively involved at the governance level at the UVCA (Ukrainian Venture Capital Association). Almaz Capital invested over $50m in over 15 companies with Ukrainian founders or teams including Adjoy, Arcycle, Content Analytics, DMarket, Hover, Jelastic, Mobalytics, Maketime, Petcube, Speedsize, Starwind, and 3Dlook . Startup teams have shown amazing resiliency and despite the war Ukraine remains one of our favorite investment destinations.

filtered.company_description:
Alderway is bridging the Equity Gap for brilliant businesses in often overlooked and underappreciated Backbone Sectors by providing the investment and support needed to unlock remarkable returns. 
 
We're a team of experienced investors, entrepreneurs and operators who have been involved in growing many successful businesses, including the groundbreaking success of Vanarama - the only vehicle leasing company to achieve an exit to a FTSE 100 giant. This is how we know building a successful business can be challenging but also very rewarding.
 
Our unique investing approach, Partnership Capital, ensures alignment of interests between us and the people we back. We not only provide financial investment but also offer support through our Value Creation Programme, empowering businesses to achieve their growth ambitions.
 
We Empower not Impose. We take a significant shareholding in the business, ensuring we have 'skin in the game,' while working alongside entrepreneurs. 
 
Our investments range from £3m to £25m, providing enough capital to fuel growth plans. 
 
If appropriate, we can facilitate early liquidity for entrepreneurs, allowing them to de-risk, and  focus on the mission rather than the mortgage while retaining a substantial stake for potential exit events. 

We invest only in businesses where our experience and guidance can accelerate growth. 
 
Our investment approach is Fundamentals Focused, emphasising profitability, cash generation, and a clean balance sheet.
 
Our ideal partners are innovative, scalable, lower mid-market (£10m - £50m revenue) and businesses not limited to the SE & London. 

By focusing on these fundamentals and partnering with businesses in Backbone Sectors, we aim to drive sustainable growth and deliver remarkable returns for all.

CEO (former) & Founder of Vanarama.
Exited following £200m sale to Auto Trader in June 22
Now backing and investing in SME’s as Founder & Managing Partner of Alderway Partnership Capital
firms.Description:
Alderway is bridging the Equity Gap for brilliant businesses in often overlooked and underappreciated Backbone Sectors by providing the investment and support needed to unlock remarkable returns. 
 
We're a team of experienced investors, entrepreneurs and operators who have been involved in growing many successful businesses, including the groundbreaking success of Vanarama - the only vehicle leasing company to achieve an exit to a FTSE 100 giant. This is how we know building a successful business can be challenging but also very rewarding.
 
Our unique investing approach, Partnership Capital, ensures alignment of interests between us and the people we back. We not only provide financial investment but also offer support through our Value Creation Programme, empowering businesses to achieve their growth ambitions.
 
We Empower not Impose. We take a significant shareholding in the business, ensuring we have 'skin in the game,' while working alongside entrepreneurs. 
 
Our investments range from £3m to £25m, providing enough capital to fuel growth plans. 
 
If appropriate, we can facilitate early liquidity for entrepreneurs, allowing them to de-risk, and  focus on the mission rather than the mortgage while retaining a substantial stake for potential exit events. 

We invest only in businesses where our experience and guidance can accelerate growth. 
 
Our investment approach is Fundamentals Focused, emphasising profitability, cash generation, and a clean balance sheet.
 
Our ideal partners are innovative, scalable, lower mid-market (£10m - £50m revenue) and businesses not limited to the SE & London. 

By focusing on these fundamentals and partnering with businesses in Backbone Sectors, we aim to drive sustainable growth and deliver remarkable returns for all.

MS+PARTNERS is a thematic private equity firm investing in growth-stage businesses with measurable, outsized impact potential. 

MS+PARTNERS was created in response to a desire to change the way capital was being allocated. It is clear to us that the sole pursuit of profit, is in itself a sub-optimal outcome for society. Our mission is to back those exceptional entrepreneurs building solutions to address the world's largest social and environmental challenges. We hope to be a catalyst that can aid in this change.

Blueverse Ventures is an award winning advisor and early stage venture investor dedicated to the #metaverse - a virtual environment where you can be physically present to hang out, play games, work and create.

At Blueverse, we are looking for early stage businesses who are building the tools and platforms across the verticals of the creator economy, discovery and experiences with a focus on the layers of infrastructure, monetisation and interface.

We are based in London, UK and our partners are global.

Winner of the "Top VC/Accelerator for Metaverse/Web3" at the European Metaverse Awards 2022.

firms.Description:
Samaipata is an early-stage founders' fund investing in digital businesses with network effects across Europe and beyond. 

The fund was founded in 2016 by two entrepreneurs, José del Barrio and Eduardo Díez-Hochleitner. José was the co-founder and CEO of the leading food-delivery platform in Spain (La Nevera Roja) which was sold for $100 million. Eduardo was founder of a 3D sound system sold to Dolby (IMM Sound), former Partner at Apax Partners, a prolific business angel, and the current Chairman at MásMóvil, recently valued at €20 billion, becoming the first Southern European Decacorn.

If you're an early-stage founder aiming at changing the world through innovation, get in touch with us! >> https://samaipata.typeform.com/to/k43fN6?utm_source=samaipata-linkedin

firms.Description:
We are an early-stage venture capital fund for founders, backed by founders. 

We set up PROfounders in 2009 to invest in and support the next wave of entrepreneurs. We believe that investing is more than just a financial commitment – we aim to be partners through the hard times as well as the good times. 

Our focus is on European companies with disruptive models that leverage technology to transform and improve customer experiences. We invest at early venture phase - when the company is typically raising £/€ 1-3m to scale.  

We know that capital is a scarce resource. We also know that time is something that you can never have enough of. We respect the time of all entrepreneurs and we aim to be as efficient and productive with your time as possible, whether you are just setting out on the journey, or looking to raise money, or part of the portfolio.
